_meta.description: Renewable energy section registered input nodes. These user-editable values define onsite and offsite renewable energy generation. All are C-classified (model-specific) and feed into net energy and emissions calculations by subtracting from total energy consumption.

instances.label: Photovoltaics
instances.note: Annual onsite photovoltaic generation. Subtracted from electricity consumption.

instances.label: Wind
instances.note: Annual onsite wind generation. Subtracted from electricity consumption.

instances.label: EV Charging Removal
instances.note: Energy attributed to EV charging, removed from building energy total.

instances.label: WWS Electricity
instances.note: Wind, water, solar offsite renewable electricity procurement.

instances.label: Green Gas
instances.note: Renewable natural gas (RNG) volume. Subtracted from gas consumption.

instances.label: Exterior/Site Loads
instances.note: Exterior and site energy loads (parking, landscaping, etc.). Removed from building total.

signature.inputs: These ARE the inputs (registered via graph.registerInput in RenewableNodes.js)
signature.output: Values stored in MultiModelState, consumed by renewable subtotal, net energy, and emissions calculations

preconditions.description: Renewable generation and removals cannot be negative

behavior.output: Triggers recomputation of onsite renewable subtotal and net electricity
Triggers recomputation of offsite renewable subtotal
behavior.note: Does not affect onsite generation metrics

behavior.output: Triggers recomputation of net gas consumption and gas emissions

behavior.condition: EV removal (d_46) or exterior loads (m_43) change
behavior.output: Triggers recomputation of adjusted building energy total

edgeCases.handling: Net consumption can go negative (net exporter)
edgeCases.rationale: Building produces more energy than it consumes

edgeCases.handling: No renewable offset applied, gross = net energy
edgeCases.rationale: Building fully reliant on grid/fossil energy

references.source: EmissionsNodes.js (consumes renewable values for net energy calculations)
